name: GPT-5.5
archDisplay: Undisclosed architecture (presumed dense transformer, reasoning-tuned); codename "Spud"; Thinking + Pro tiers
note: Codename Spud; ChatGPT/Codex launched Apr 23, 2026 with API following ~1 day later once safeguards were finalized.

results.notes: SWE-Bench Pro, base GPT-5.5 (not Pro variant); confirmed via marktechpost.com direct report
Terminal-Bench 2.0, new SOTA at launch; confirmed
FrontierMath Tier 1-3, base model (Pro scores 51.0-52.4 across sources); confirmed
FrontierMath Tier 4, base model (Pro scores 39.6); confirmed

GPQA Diamond score for base GPT-5.5, explicitly labeled 'OpenAI self-reported (launch post)' by llm-stats.com's research comparison; independently corroborated at the identical 93.6% by buildfastwithai's GPT-5.5 review (2026) and by benchlm.ai's GPQA-D leaderboard. Not previously in this corpus entry.

Humanity's Last Exam, no tools, base GPT-5.5. Explicitly labeled 'OpenAI self-reported (launch post)' by llm-stats.com; identical figure independently corroborated by buildfastwithai's review. Distinct from GPT-5.5 Pro's HLE-with-tools figure of 57.2% (a different setting, not added here).

OSWorld-Verified for base GPT-5.5, labeled OpenAI self-reported launch-post figure by llm-stats.com; independently corroborated at the identical 78.7% by kingy.ai's 'quoted verbatim from OpenAI's evaluation tables' rundown and by buildfastwithai's review.

Tau2-bench Telecom, 'without any prompt tuning' per OpenAI's own framing, base GPT-5.5. Labeled OpenAI self-reported launch-post figure; identical figure independently corroborated by interestingengineering.com's GPT-5.5 coverage and the WebSearch synopsis of OpenAI's own launch page.

ARC-AGI-2, base GPT-5.5, listed by kingy.ai as one of the '9 numbers ... quoted verbatim from OpenAI's evaluation tables'; independently corroborated at the identical 85.0% by buildfastwithai's review and by benchlm.ai's dedicated ARC-AGI-2 leaderboard ('GPT-5.5 Leads at 85%'). Flagging that this exceeds the taxonomy's documented arc-agi-2 range ceiling of 82 -- multiple independent sources agree on 85.0% as a new-SOTA figure at launch, so the taxonomy range appears to be outdated rather than the score being wrong, but noting this for the ingesting pipeline's awareness.

verificationNotes:
Existence, April 23 2026 release, codename Spud, 1.05M context window, and $5/$30 pricing all confirmed via multiple independent outlets (Axios, Wikipedia, MarkTechPost) plus developers.openai.com model docs. All seven benchmark scores (SWE-Bench Pro 58.6, Terminal-Bench 82.7, GDPval 84.9, BrowseComp 84.4, FrontierMath T1-3 51.7, FrontierMath T4 35.4, CyberGym 81.8) independently corroborated as base-model (not Pro-variant) figures across MarkTechPost and other launch-day coverage.

GAP-RESOLUTION PASS (2026-07-16): Resolved all 7 flagged core-benchmark gaps (mmlu, math, aime, humaneval, livecodebench, swe-bench, mmmu). Checked OpenAI's own materials first -- fetched the launch post plus the complete GPT-5.5 System Card PDF directly from deploymentsafety.openai.com/gpt-5-5/gpt-5-5.pdf (48 pages, full table of contents reviewed): it covers safety, robustness, health, hallucinations, alignment, bias, and preparedness evaluations only -- no MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval/LiveCodeBench/MMMU capability-benchmark section exists in OpenAI's own GPT-5.5 documentation at all. Then checked two independent trackers directly (not just search snippets): Epoch AI (rendered epoch.ai/models/gpt-5-5 in-browser and separately pulled the raw epoch.ai/data/benchmarks.csv export, confirming a single canonical 'GPT-5.5' model entity for the internally-run evals, not mixed across the page's reasoning-effort-tier selector) and Vals AI (fetched raw HTML of vals.ai/models/openai_gpt-5.5 and parsed the embedded data-target score attributes, since the rendered page shows misleading '0.0%' placeholders pre-animation).

ADDED: (1) swe-bench = 80.6% from Epoch AI's internal SWE-bench Verified run (484/500 human-validated samples; this corpus's own 'swe-bench' slug is display-named 'SWE-bench Verified' in benchmarks.json, so this is the correct fit; cross-checked against Vals AI's independent measurement of 82.6% for the same subset, ~2pp cross-evaluator variance, expected and not a conflict). (2) livecodebench = 85.3% from Vals AI (page titled plainly 'LiveCodeBench', not a mislabeled variant; not evaluated by Epoch AI for this model and not published by OpenAI).

OMITTED (genuine, checked absences, not search failures): (3) mmlu -- absent from OpenAI's materials; Epoch AI's own external_benchmarks/mmlu.csv legacy dataset (fetched directly) has zero GPT-5.x-era rows; Vals AI's GPT-5.5 table has MMLU-Pro only, no plain MMLU. This corpus's own catalog already flags MMLU as 'largely saturated at the frontier.' (4) math (MATH-500) -- absent from the System Card; Epoch AI's math/science table for GPT-5.5 has FrontierMath/OTIS-Mock-AIME/ProofBench/CritPt/GPQA but no MATH-500 row, and epoch.ai/data/external_benchmarks/math.csv 404s; Vals AI doesn't list it. (5) aime -- the real AIME is absent from OpenAI's own GPT-5.5 materials (unlike GPT-5, GPT-5.2, and GPT-5 Pro, which did publish real AIME numbers already in this corpus, the GPT-5.4/5.4-Pro/5.5/5.6 generation stopped reporting it) and absent from Vals AI. Epoch AI does report 'OTIS Mock AIME 2024-2025' at 100.0% for GPT-5.5 -- deliberately NOT mapped onto this corpus's 'aime' slug, following the identical precedent already established in corpus/models/anthropic/claude-sonnet-5.json: OTIS Mock AIME is a distinct, contamination-resistant proxy exam written in AIME's style rather than real AIME questions, and mapping it onto the real-AIME slug would misrepresent comparability against other models' genuine-AIME pass@1 scores. Downstream aggregators (themodelbeat.com) that relabel this same Epoch figure as plain 'AIME 2024/2025' are committing exactly this mislabeling error and were not used as a workaround. (6) humaneval -- absent from OpenAI's materials and from Epoch AI's coding-benchmark table for GPT-5.5 (SciCode/FrontierSWE/DeepSWE/CursorBench/FrontierCode/SWE-bench-Verified/ALE-Bench/Text-Arena, no HumanEval; epoch.ai/data/external_benchmarks/humaneval.csv 404s); absent from Vals AI. This corpus's own catalog already flags HumanEval as 'long-saturated at the frontier.' (7) mmmu (base, non-Pro) -- OpenAI's launch table published MMMU-Pro (81.2%, self-reported, corroborated across three independent citations as one of the numbers 'quoted verbatim' from the official table) but never plain MMMU; Epoch AI doesn't track MMMU at all for this model (multimodal table has only Blueprint-Bench-2 and GDP.pdf); Vals AI's model page shows a tile labeled 'MMMU' at 88.27%, but directly fetching vals.ai/benchmarks/mmmu confirms the page's own <title> is 'MMMU Pro' -- i.e. Vals AI's 'MMMU' tile is MMMU-Pro under a legacy URL slug, the identical mislabeling already documented for this exact tracker in the claude-sonnet-5.json precedent -- so it was correctly NOT added under this corpus's separate base-'mmmu' slug (that would double-count an MMMU-Pro score as base MMMU; this base gpt-5-5.json entry doesn't currently carry an mmmu-pro entry either, and adding one is out of scope for this pass, which is scoped to the 7 flagged core-slug gaps only). Rejected as unreliable and not used for any addition: automatio.ai, startupfortune.com, enter.converge.ai, and tokenmix.ai all publish full MMLU/MATH/HumanEval/SWE-bench tables for GPT-5.5 with zero cited sources and internally inconsistent numbers across sites (MMLU 92.4% vs 92.5%; 'SWE-Bench' 88.7% vs the verified self-reported/independent range of 58.6-82.6%) -- classic uncited SEO-farm content.

DEEP RESEARCH PASS ROUND 2 (2026-07-17): Targeted re-chase of the 4 still-missing core slugs (mmlu, math, aime, humaneval) using 6 source types the prior pass had NOT used (that pass covered: official launch page, System Card PDF, Epoch AI, Vals AI, LMArena main page, BenchLM, llm-stats.com). All 6 new source types were genuinely checked; none surfaced a credible, independently-citable score for any of the 4 gaps.

(1) Stanford HELM: fetched crfm.stanford.edu/helm/lite/latest, /classic/latest, and /capabilities pages directly, plus searched the stanford-crfm/helm GitHub repo's model registry for 'gpt-5.5'. No GPT-5.5 entry exists on any HELM leaderboard. Search results independently confirm HELM entered maintenance mode on June 1, 2026 -- only ~5 weeks after GPT-5.5's April 23, 2026 release -- explaining why it was never added; HELM's most recently indexed OpenAI entries are from the GPT-4o generation.

(2) arXiv / Semantic Scholar: ran multiple targeted searches (site:arxiv.org "GPT-5.5" MMLU; "GPT-5.5" baseline comparison MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval; semanticscholar.org "GPT-5.5" evaluation table 2026). Found exactly one paper referencing GPT-5.5 at all in this context -- "Beyond Benchmarks: MathArena as an Evaluation Platform for Mathematics with LLMs" (arxiv.org/pdf/2605.00674) -- which cites the GPT-5.5 System Card's existence but reports no actual MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval score for it. No paper anywhere in these searches reports a numeric baseline score for GPT-5.5 on any of the 4 target benchmarks.

(3) GitHub eval-harness logs: searched EleutherAI/lm-evaluation-harness (and forks) for committed results.json artifacts referencing gpt-5.5. The harness supports OpenAI-API-backed model evaluation generically, but no committed run output specific to GPT-5.5 was found in any repo.

(4) Other leaderboards/aggregators: OpenRouter's model page and dedicated /benchmarks page (openrouter.ai/openai/gpt-5.5, /benchmarks) show only pricing/throughput/uptime -- no MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval breakdown at all. Artificial Analysis (artificialanalysis.ai/models/gpt-5-5 and /gpt-5-5-high, fetched directly) confirmed its Intelligence Index has structurally DROPPED all 4 classic benchmarks, replaced by GDPval-AA v2, tau3-Banking, Terminal-Bench v2.1, SciCode, Humanity's Last Exam, GPQA Diamond, CritPt, AA-Omniscience, AA-LCR, and MMMU-Pro -- this independently corroborates, from a brand-new source, this file's prior-pass observation that the industry has moved off these 4 legacy benchmarks for frontier-tier models. Scale AI SEAL leaderboard tests an entirely different task set (SWE-bench-style code, Spanish-language, instruction-following, grade-school math) and only shows GPT-5.4 (not 5.5) at 59.1% on its standardized board -- not a comparable metric. PapersWithCode searches surfaced no dedicated GPT-5.5 leaderboard row for any of the 4 benchmarks. LMArena category leaderboards (Math Arena, Coding Arena) report Elo ratings, not percentage benchmark scores, so even where GPT-5.5 appears there it isn't a unit-comparable figure for these slugs.

(5) Non-English sources: checked Chinese-language coverage (Zhihu-indexed GPT-5.5 reviews) and Japanese-language coverage (note.com, Qiita, CodeRabbit's Japanese blog). Chinese sources report only a vague relative claim ("~12% average improvement over GPT-5 on GPQA/ARC-C/MMLU-Pro composite") with no absolute plain-MMLU number. Japanese sources report unrelated custom benchmarks (CodeRabbit's own issue-detection-rate eval, a KernelGen score) with no MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval figures anywhere in either language's community coverage.

(6) Sibling models: checked GPT-5.6 (the next model in the family, launched July 9 2026) launch coverage and dedicated GPT-5.6-vs-GPT-5.5 comparison pieces (aigptjournal.com, artificialanalysis.ai/articles/gpt-5-6-has-landed) for a comparison table that might carry GPT-5.5's classic-benchmark numbers as the baseline column. aigptjournal.com explicitly states GPT-5.6 itself "did not publish classic academic benchmarks (GPQA/MMLU/AIME/SWE-bench Verified)" either -- confirming the entire 5.x-late-generation stopped reporting these, so no sibling-model table exists to cross-reference GPT-5.5's figures from either.

Near-miss explicitly rejected: llmreference.com's GLM-5-vs-GPT-5.5 comparison page (fetched directly) listed AIME 2025 = 81.2 for GPT-5.5, alongside GPQA 93.6 and HLE 41.4 that match this corpus's already-confirmed self-reported values. However the page states only "data sourced from public model cards and provider documentation" with zero per-number citations or links, and the 81.2 AIME figure is not corroborated by any other source encountered in either research pass (OpenAI's own materials, Epoch AI, and Vals AI were all already confirmed to lack real-AIME data for GPT-5.5). This matches the exact uncited-SEO-aggregator pattern the first pass already rejected for automatio.ai/startupfortune.com/tokenmix.ai/enter.converge.ai (e.g. those sites' MMLU 92.4-92.5% claims, also surfaced again fresh in this pass' Chinese/Japanese/general searches, still trace to the same uncited outlets) -- not used.

CONCLUSION: All 6 newly-specified source types were genuinely checked (exceeding the required minimum of 3), and none yielded a credible, independently-citable MMLU, MATH, AIME, or HumanEval score for base GPT-5.5. This reinforces rather than contradicts the first pass's conclusion. Recommend continuing to treat all 4 as omitted_no_evidence; this looks like a durable absence (whole-generation self-reporting shift, confirmed now by Artificial Analysis's own benchmark-suite redesign and GPT-5.6's continued non-reporting of the same 4 benchmarks) rather than a search-coverage gap, unless some future independent evaluator runs its own eval against the GPT-5.5 API specifically for these legacy benchmarks.
